hallucination.rationale: The note contains massive fabrications with no basis in the transcript: hypertension, diabetes, metformin, smoking history, alcohol use, occupation as a nurse, vital signs (BP 130/80, HR 110, etc.), skin ultrasound, height/weight, and an entirely fabricated Review of Systems including fever, chills, chest pain, palpitations, edema, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, joint pain, headaches, mood swings, and many more.
flagged_content: She has had hypertension for several years
She has also had a history of diabetes and has been on metformin for diabetes management
She is taking metformin 500mg twice daily
She has been a heavy smoker for most of her life
She has been drinking moderately
She works as a nurse
Temp: 98.6
RR: 20
She is 5'8" tall and has a normal weight
The patient has constitutional issues, including fever, chills, fatigue, and weight changes
She also reports vision and hearing issues, throat and nasal symptoms
she has cardiovascular issues, including chest pain and palpitations, and has edema
she has gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, and has had diarrhea
She also reports joint pain and muscle weakness
The neurological section mentions headaches and dizziness
the psychiatric section mentions mood swings and anxiety
We will have a skin ultrasound to evaluate the mole
The primary diagnosis is a benign mole

instruction_following.rationale: The note retains template bracketed instructions in the output (e.g., '[Patient's primary reason for visit]', '[Detailed description of current problem...]'). The ROS section was supposed to be omitted if not discussed, but instead the note fabricated an entire ROS. The note also fabricated content instead of stating 'Not documented' for sections like Past Medical History, Medications, Social History, and Family History.

completeness.rationale: The note captures some key elements (changing mole, bleeding episode, excisional biopsy scheduled Thursday at 2:30, pathology in 5-7 days) but misses many important details including the ABCDE criteria findings, dermatoscope use, lidocaine anesthesia, suture removal in 10-14 days, instructions to avoid aspirin/ibuprofen, Tylenol being acceptable, wound care instructions, sunscreen counseling (SPF 30+), annual full skin exams recommendation, and the patient's fair skin/poor sun protection history.
missed_items: Detailed ABCDE criteria findings (asymmetry, irregular borders, variegated color, 8mm diameter)
Avoid aspirin and ibuprofen for 3 days prior
Keep site clean and dry for 24 hours post-procedure
Sunscreen counseling SPF 30+, reapply every 2 hours
Monthly self-skin checks with husband's help
Fair-skinned with poor sun protection history
Possible outcomes discussion (atypical benign vs melanoma, wider excision, referral to surgical oncology)
Doctor will call personally with results

template_adherence.rationale: The note follows the general section structure of the template but retains bracketed/parenthetical template instructions in the output, adds an unauthorized 'Conclusion' section, and includes fabricated sections that should have been omitted or marked 'Not documented'.

entity_marking.rationale: No entity marking was attempted anywhere in the note. No {{drug:...}} or {{condition:...}} syntax was used.

duplication.rationale: The follow-up instructions are duplicated in Plan items 3 and 4, and the Conclusion section repeats information already stated in the Plan and Assessment.
